Value: 219","CreationDate":"2025-12-12","SUBJECT":"EDUCATION, WALES","CATEGORY":"EDUCATION, WALES","SUMMARY":"These Regulations apply to local authorities and governing bodies that provide food and drink on a school day to registered pupils at maintained primary schools, whether the food and drink is provided on school premises or not. For the purposes of these Regulations, “maintained primary school” means a maintained school that provides primary education (whether or not it also provides other kinds of education). “Maintained school” means a community, foundation or voluntary school, a community special school or a pupil referral unit in Wales or a maintained nursery school as defined in regulation 2(1). Where a maintained primary school also provides secondary education, these Regulations will apply only to food and drink provided to registered pupils in year 6 and below.","":""}

content: tempt to mimic meat or fish products, such as mycoprotein sausages and burgers and plant based sausages and burgers but not including soya, mycoprotein or pea protein mince, chunks or pieces; “ pupil ” (“ disgybl ”) has the meaning given to it by section 3(1) of the 1996 Act( 9 ); “ pupil referral unit ” (“ uned cyfeirio disgyblion ”) has the meaning given to it by section 19A of the 1996 Act( 10 ); “ reception year ” (“ blwyddyn derbyn ”) means a year group in which the majority of pupils attain the age of 5; “ red meat ” (“ cig coch ”) means meat of mammalian species including beef, goat meat, lamb, mutton, pork, veal and venison but not including rabbit meat; “ registered pupil ” (“ disgybl cofrestredig ”) means a child registered as a pupil at a maintained primary school in a register kept under section 434 of the 1996 Act; “ residential trip ” (“ taith breswyl ”) has the meaning given to it by section 462(2) of the 1996 Act( 11 ); “ sandwiches ” (“ brechdanau ”) includes filled rolls and similar products which are ready to eat without further preparation; “ savoury snacks ” (“ byrbrydau sawrus ”) means pre-packaged savoury items that are ready to eat without further preparation and consist of or include as a basic ingredient potatoes or other root vegetables, cereals, legumes, nuts or seeds, but does not include— (a) legumes, nuts, seeds or root vegetables with no added fat, salt or sugar, (b) plain unflavoured crackers, oatcakes or water biscuits, or (c) sandwiches; “ school breakfast ” (“ brecwast ysgol ”) means food and drink provided for consumption by pupils on a school day before the start of the morning school session, or in the case of a community special school before or at the start of the morning school session; “ school day ” (“ diwrnod ysgol ”), in relation to a maintained primary school, means any day on which at that school there is at least one school session; “ school lunch ” (“ cinio ysgol ”) means food and drink provided for consumption by pupils as their midday meal on a school day, whether involving a set meal or the selection of items by them; “ school premises ” (“ mangre ysgol ”) means the premises of a maintained primary school; “ school session ” (“ sesiwn ysgol ”), in relation to a maintained primary school, means a school session beginning and ending at such times as may be determined from time to time for that school in accordance with section 32C of the Education Act 2002( 12 ); “ school year ” (“ blwyddyn ysgol ”) has the meaning given to it by section 579(1) of the 1996 Act( 13 ); “ secondary education ” (“ addysg uwchradd ”) has the meaning given to it by section 2(2) of the 1996 Act; “ specified meat product ” (“ cynnyrch cig penodedig ”) has the meaning given to it by Schedule 1; “ supplementary bread ” (“ bara atodol ”) means bread that is not part of a sandwich or of a set meal; “ sweetened baked products and desserts ” (“ cynhyrchion pob wedi eu melysu a phwdinau wedi eu melysu ”) includes sweet pastry and sweet products containing pastry but does not include ambient or chilled yoghurt; “ vegetables ” (“ llysiau ”) includes salad but does not include potatoes; “ week ” (“ wythnos ”) means the five days from Monday to Friday; “ wholegrain ” (“ grawn cyflawn ”) includes wholemeal; “ year 1 ” (“ blwyddyn 1 ”) means a year group in which the majority of pupils attain the age of 6; “ year 2 ” (“ blwyddyn 2 ”) means a year group in which the majority of pupils attain the age of 7; “ year 3 ” (“ blwyddyn 3 ”) means a year group in which the majority of pupils attain the age of 8; “ year 4 ” (“ blwyddyn 4 ”) means a year group in which the majority of pupils attain the age of 9; “ year 5 ” (“ blwyddyn 5 ”) means a year group in which the majority of pupils attain the age of 10; “ year 6 ” (“ blwyddyn 6 ”) means a year group in which the majority of pupils attain the age of 11; “ year group ” (“ grŵp blwyddyn ”) means a group of pupils at a maintained primary school the majority of whom will, in a particular school year, attain the same age. (2) Nothing in these Regulations applies to food or drink provided— (a) at fund-raising events;  (b) for use in teaching food or drink preparation and cookery skills provided that any food or drink so prepared is not provided to registered pupils as part of a school breakfast or school lunch;  (c) as part of any medically prescribed dietary requirements;  (d) to registered pupils at a boarding school as part of an evening meal;  (e) to registered pupils on a residential trip;  (f) to registered pupils on the premises of a maintained school that does not provide primary education.  (3) These Regulations do not apply to food and drink provided to mark any religious or cultural occasion other than when the food and drink is provided as a school lunch. (4) Where a maintained primary school provides primary and secondary education, any reference in regulations 3 to 5 to a registered pupil is a reference to an infant pupil or a junior pupil. (5) References in these Regulations to food or drink provided by a local authority or the governing body of a maintained primary school include food or drink provided in pursuance of an agreement or other arrangement made by such an authority or body for the provision of food or drink. (6) In the definition of “maintained school” in paragraph (1), the following expressions have the same meaning as given by section 20 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998( 14 )— (a) community school;  (b) foundation school;  (c) voluntary school;  (d) community special school.  School breakfast in maintained primary schools 3. —(1) This regulation applies where a local authority or a governing body of a maintained primary school provides school breakfast to registered pupils whether on school premises or at a place other than school premises. (2) The local authority or governing body must ensure that the requirements in Schedule 2 are complied with. School lunch in maintained primary schools 4. —(1) This regulation applies where a local authority or a governing body of a maintained primary school provides a school lunch to registered pupils whether on school premises or at a place other than school premises. (2) The local authority or governing body must ensure that the requirements in Schedule 3 and the nutritional standards in Schedule 5 are complied with. (3) But where a school lunch referred to in paragraph (1) is provided to mark any religious or cultural occasion, paragraph (2) applies but as if “and the nutritional standards in Schedule 5” were omitted. (4) Where a school is open for fewer than five days in any week the requirements in paragraphs 2(2), 3(2), 5(2) and (3), 7(1), 8(1) and 11(1) of Schedule 3 do not apply. Provision of other food or drink in maintained primary schools 5. —(1) This regulation applies where a local authority or a governing body of a maintained primary school provides food or drink before 6pm on a school day otherwise than as part of a school breakfast or a school lunch to registered pupils whether on school premises or at a place other than school premises. (2) The local authority or governing body must ensure that the requirements in Schedule 4 are complied with. Minor and consequential amendments to The Healthy Eating in Schools (Nutritional Standards and Requirements) (Wales) Regulations 2013 6. The Healthy Eating in Schools (Nutritional Standards and Requirements) (Wales) Regulations 2013( 15 ) are amended as set out in Schedule 6. Lynne Neagle Cabinet Secretary for Education, one of the Welsh Ministers 12 December 2025 Regulation 2 SCHEDULE 1 Specified meat product 1. —(1) A specified meat product is a food falling within sub-paragraph (2)
